mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate remains stable or declines below 4.3%, indicating robust labor market conditions that sustain consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE < 4.3%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) accelerates above the 3-month moving average, signaling strengthening consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 3-month moving average by >0.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 1.5% over three months while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5%, signaling stagflationary pressure that reduces real corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < -1.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint effect of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL interaction term has t-statistic < 1.5 in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1200 thousand units while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth slows below 100k per month, indicating weakening demand momentum that presages earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: demand deceleration with labor market softening
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between HOUST and PAYEMS growth becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 150k per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines, signaling tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate cost inflation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150k AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage acceleration risk
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of strong payroll growth and declining unemployment shows Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth declines below 3.0% while federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, indicating the Fed has achieved disinflation without recession, supporting equity valuations through lower terminal rate expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the correlation between disinflation progress and forward returns becomes negative and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling strengthening productive capacity and supply-side expansion that supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% in current and prior month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
